mechanism_narrative: A persistent federal funds rate held near cycle highs continues to raise the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, exerting continuous downward pressure on S&P 500 valuations independent of any new trigger event. This always-on drag manifests as negative forward returns over a one-month horizon whenever FEDFUNDS remains elevated relative to its trailing 12-month average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or forward returns during high-rate periods are not significantly more negative than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, causing equity markets to reprice growth expectations downward over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC conditional on trigger activation is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a low or falling federal funds rate jointly signal robust consumer demand unconstrained by tight monetary policy, producing a reinforcing bullish effect on equities that neither series alone captures, since strong sales amid tightening policy or weak sales amid easing policy do not carry the same signal strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND FEDFUNDS falling or flat over trailing 3 months
regime_hint: monetary easing with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows 20-day forward return distribution statistically indistinguishable from single-factor RSAFS-only signal (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10) across a 3-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL is running above target-consistent levels while the federal funds rate is also elevated, the market treats further inflation surprises as forcing additional tightening, so rising CPI readings compress forward returns; but in a low-rate regime the same CPI increase is read as reflation and supports equities. The direction of the CPI effect flips depending on the prevailing FEDFUNDS regime.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: The sign of the CPI-return relationship fails to flip between high-FEDFUNDS and low-FEDFUNDS subsamples, or Rank IC in the high-rate regime is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects sustained expansion in real economic output and corporate earnings capacity, and this positive trend translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week regardless of any specific triggering threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing growth rate and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A month in which nonfarm payroll growth decelerates sharply, falling below a threshold consistent with stalling job creation, signals an abrupt weakening in labor demand that markets have not yet priced, leading to negative equity returns over the following month as growth expectations are revised down.
regime_hint: expansion phase nearing labor market inflection
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following triggered low-payroll months are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (p > 0.10) once revision-adjusted PAYEMS values are used.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator of consumer confidence and interest-rate-sensitive investment; during periods when the federal funds rate is historically high, a decline in HOUST signals contracting housing demand that foreshadows broader economic slowing and depresses equity returns over the following quarter. In low-rate regimes, HOUST fluctuations carry weaker predictive power because housing activity is less rate-constrained.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: In the high-rate regime, 60-day forward returns following a HOUST decline trigger are not significantly more negative than unconditional returns (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and the federal funds rate is simultaneously high, the market interprets this combination as a signal that the central bank has more room to tighten further to cool an overheating labor market, producing negative equity returns over the very short term (1 week) as rate-hike expectations are priced in. Neither low unemployment nor high rates alone reliably produces this short-horizon effect.
regime_hint: tight labor market during monetary tightening
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on the joint trigger are not significantly different from returns conditional on FEDFUNDS alone (interaction coefficient p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.
